column9: Newtonian at high water content (espresso regime); becomes power-law only below ~64% water (>36% solids) - far above espresso TDS

notes: espresso TDS ~4-12% solids = 88-96% water = firmly Newtonian; no non-Newtonian correction needed for espresso liquor
density rises with solids, falls with T; espresso low-TDS end -> near pure water (~960-1000 at brew temp)
consistent with Telis-Romero; measured near freezing so T-range not espresso-relevant, density magnitudes cross-check only
density depends more strongly on solid content than on temperature; R2=0.989 for their fitted model
pure water at 90C ~0.315 mPa*s; current repo uses this everywhere (MANIFEST waszkiewicz2025/constants: mu=pure-water@90C)
pure water at 25C for comparison / rheometer conditions

SUPERSEDED guess (kept for provenance): pre-transcription snippet estimate ~1.3-2x pure water. Now corrected by the transcribed Eq-10 closure -> see viscosity_espresso_estimate_closure row and telisromero2001_closures.csv. The 1.3-2x magnitude belongs to concentrated early IN-PORE liquor (X_w~76), not bulk shot TDS.

CLOSURE-DERIVED (data.telisromero_viscosity_pas(363,90)): bulk shot-TDS mu ~= 1.06x pure water @90C = NEGLIGIBLE Darcy correction. Reproduces authors' Table-1 anchor within 1.5%. Espresso still an extrapolation toward water (composition + dilute-end caveat).
mu(T) follows Arrhenius exp(Ea/RT); Ea and pre-exponential are functions of water content in their 5-parameter fit
pseudoplastic+thixotropic above 36% solids; also swelling-driven viscosity creep on storage/heating - irrelevant to fresh espresso liquor
